cityDNA.coreFantasy: Volgrad is a frost-gate Slavic civic city where guild craft, cold law, archives, refugee care, and protected nurseries hold a severe frontier society together.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Volgrad should feel immediately distinct: Eastern European / Slavic civic life is organized around Artisan Guilds, High Residential, Records / Archives, Refugee Commons, and Nursery / Spawning Grounds, with Eastern Watchfire Kremlin of the Frost Gate as the citywide symbol.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: The city turns Blue Granite, Frost Glass, Pine, Silver Inlay, Arcane Glow, Dry Stone Paving, Civic Terraces, and Civic Blue Illumination, Oath Seals, Weather Binding into a working urban system rather than surface decoration.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Use Blue Granite, Frost Glass, Pine, Silver Inlay, Arcane Glow, civic terraces, public courts, audience terraces, sealed archives, command platforms, Artisan Guilds Civic Hall, High Residential Civic Hall, Records / Archives Hall, visible Guildhall Beacon, the central Frost Rune Edges Sovereign Registry Court, and the skyline-scale Eastern Watchfire Kremlin of the Frost Gate as the core visual grammar.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A Eastern European / Slavic population whose institutions make daily life legible through section anchors: Artisan Guilds, High Residential, Records / Archives, Refugee Commons, Nursery / Spawning Grounds.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: The city is symbolized by Eastern Watchfire Kremlin of the Frost Gate, Guildhall Beacon, Frost Rune Edges Sovereign Registry Court, and the repeating civic pattern of hall, landmark, monument, and nook in every section.

canonLocks: Volgrad is built for its Eastern European / Slavic population and should not be genericized into a neutral fantasy city.
The Wonder is Eastern Watchfire Kremlin of the Frost Gate.
The Seat of Power is Frost Rune Edges Sovereign Registry Court.
The five section spines are Artisan Guilds, High Residential, Records / Archives, Refugee Commons, Nursery / Spawning Grounds.
The tactical board uses 25 district slots, with District 13 as the seat-of-power center.
Exact sidecar names for civic halls, major landmarks, monuments, and minor nooks must be preserved verbatim.
provisionalCanon: Citizens likely treat Guildhall Beacon as the practical public signal point for citywide alerts and Conjunction response.
Daily politics likely moves through the named section institutions rather than through faction headquarters.
The city’s civic magic expresses through Civic Blue Illumination, Oath Seals, Weather Binding when visible in public spaces.
rejectedReads: Volgrad as a generic fantasy settlement with the culture applied as ornament only.
Replacing exact section anchors with generic labels such as market, temple, tower, archive, or court when the sidecar provides a proper name.
Treating the Wonder, Beacon, or Seat of Power as interchangeable skyline props instead of civic machinery.
cautionFlags: Do not add unsupported per-district landmark names beyond the source-backed section anchors.
Keep all 25 districts, but derive their specific identity from the five section spines unless new district-specific sidecars exist.
Preserve the material language: Blue Granite, Frost Glass, Pine, Silver Inlay, Arcane Glow.
Preserve the terrain/water logic: Dry Stone Paving, Civic Terraces; water context: dry city ground.

environment.environmentalLogic: Environment should make the city’s institutions readable: Artisan Guilds, High Residential, Records / Archives, Refugee Commons, Nursery / Spawning Grounds must feel embedded in Dry Stone Paving, Civic Terraces, with water handled as dry city ground and public magic appearing as Civic Blue Illumination, Oath Seals, Weather Binding.

skyline: Eastern Watchfire Kremlin of the Frost Gate dominates the far skyline and should be larger than every section landmark.
Frost Rune Edges Sovereign Registry Court anchors the central District 13 civic mass.
Guildhall Beacon is the section-1 signal landmark and must be readable from nearby districts.
Section skyline rhythm: Guildhall Beacon, Manor Court, Archive Tower, Aid Commons, Nursery Basin.

civilization.people: The population is Eastern European / Slavic. Civic scenes should show them using the city’s architecture naturally: gathering near Guildhall Beacon, moving through Artisan Guilds, High Residential, Records / Archives, and treating the named anchors as practical institutions.
socialClasses: seat-of-power officials attached to Frost Rune Edges Sovereign Registry Court
section hall administrators and permit keepers
craft, market, transport, religious, or military guild households depending on district spine
street vendors, carriers, maintenance workers, and household service families
visitors and petitioners who navigate the city through public anchors
civilization.governance: Governance is centered on Frost Rune Edges Sovereign Registry Court and expressed locally through the five section civic halls. Authority should be visible in courts, archives, public signals, permits, and named monuments rather than hidden abstraction.
civilization.religion: Religious and symbolic life should draw on Civic Blue Illumination, Oath Seals, Weather Binding, the Wonder’s public symbolism, and whichever section anchors carry sacred or ceremonial roles.
customs: citizens use named halls and nooks as practical meeting points
public signals from the Beacon interrupt ordinary commerce and movement
section monuments preserve civic memory and local identity
visitors orient themselves by Wonder, Beacon, Seat, and section major landmarks
formal petitions and judgments move toward District 13
bodyLanguage: citizens make room for official messengers near beacon approaches
locals gesture by landmark rather than by street name
public posture changes around courts, monuments, and halls
market or service workers keep one eye on signal points during tense periods
speechPatterns: street speech abbreviates section landmarks but not Wonder, Beacon, or Seat names
public announcements use practical civic language rather than poetic vagueness

streets.appearance: Street views should show Blue Granite, Frost Glass, Pine, Silver Inlay, Arcane Glow, civic terraces, public courts, audience terraces, sealed archives, command platforms, Artisan Guilds Civic Hall, pedestrians using section halls, and sightlines back to Guildhall Beacon or Frost Rune Edges Sovereign Registry Court where appropriate.
streets.movement: Movement is organized by section spine: Artisan Guilds, High Residential, Records / Archives, Refugee Commons, Nursery / Spawning Grounds. Districts feed toward their civic hall, major landmark, monument, and minor nook before converging on District 13 for authority scenes.
streets.lighting: Use civic blue illumination for official signal moments, practical lantern light for night scenes, and material-specific highlights on Blue Granite, Frost Glass, Pine, Silver Inlay.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should reinforce terrain and water logic: Dry Stone Paving, Civic Terraces; water context dry city ground.
streets.dayCycle: Mornings emphasize deliveries, hall openings, street maintenance, market preparation, and official notices; afternoons build toward hearings, training, guild work, or section-specific labor.
streets.nightCycle: Evenings compress around lanterns, food stalls, guarded thresholds, quieter civic errands, and beacon visibility against darker streets.

prestigious: Frost Rune Edges Sovereign Registry Court document ateliers
Eastern Watchfire Kremlin of the Frost Gate ceremonial suppliers

housing.wealthy: Residences near Frost Rune Edges Sovereign Registry Court or high-status section courts use the best Blue Granite, Frost Glass, Pine, private terraces, and controlled views of civic landmarks.
housing.middle: Households cluster above shops and along civic streets, sharing courtyards, balconies, workrooms, and access to section halls.
housing.poor: Lower-status housing is practical, crowded, and close to service yards or labor streets, but still uses local motifs and public landmarks for orientation.

wonder.name: Eastern Watchfire Kremlin of the Frost Gate
wonder.symbolicRole: The citywide symbol of Eastern European / Slavic identity made public and monumental.
wonder.visualRole: A one-per-city wonder: spectacular, unmistakable, and larger than every section landmark. It turns Volgrad's geography, race identity, material culture, and civic magic into one public symbol. Built for Volgrad's Eastern European / Slavic population, it uses Blue Granite, Frost Glass, Pine, Silver Inlay, Arcane Glow across Dry Stone Paving, Civic Terraces; water context: dry city ground; magic expression: Civic Blue
wonder.civicRole: A unifying civic symbol larger than section landmarks; it gives Volgrad its skyline identity and public myth.
storyHooks: a public ceremony at the Wonder changes city mood
maintenance access reveals hidden civic routes
the Wonder becomes the reference point during a beacon state

beacon.name: Guildhall Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: The public signal point where citywide warning becomes local action.
beacon.visualRole: A largest local landmark in this section, subordinate only to the city wonder and citadel. It belongs to Section 1 (Artisan Guilds) of Volgrad and should use Forge Halls, Market Arcades, White Limestone Civic Blocks, pine and silver inlay materials, Lantern Lit, Woven Banners, Red Fabric Awnings, and the Eastern European / Slavic visual language. Design focus: distinct gameplay landmark, visible from nearby districts
beacon.civicRole: The Section 1 signal landmark; citizens watch it for alerts, public notices, and Conjunction response.
storyHooks: a signal is interpreted before officials arrive
crowds block the approach
the beacon reveals which district reacts first

seatOfPower.name: Frost Rune Edges Sovereign Registry Court
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Civic authority made visible through courts, archives, command platforms, and public legitimacy.
seatOfPower.visualRole: The district-13 seat of power: courts, audience terraces, sealed archives, command platforms, and the visible apparatus of government. It is not a faction HQ; it is the city's central civic authority. Built for Volgrad's Eastern European / Slavic population, it uses Blue Granite, Frost Glass, Pine, Silver Inlay, Arcane Glow across Dry Stone Paving, Civic Terraces; water context: dry city ground; magic expression: Civ
seatOfPower.civicRole: District 13 authority center for petitions, archives, judgments, and city-scale decisions; not a faction headquarters.
storyHooks: a sealed record contradicts public belief
a petition exposes citywide risk
command platforms become crowd-facing stages

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 / Artisan Guilds must preserve Artisan Guilds Civic Hall, Guildhall Beacon, Woven Banners Monument, and Masterwork Arcade Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.
S2 / High Residential must preserve High Residential Civic Hall, Manor Court, Hanging Gardens Monument, and Salon Pavilion Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.
S3 / Records / Archives must preserve Records / Archives Hall, Archive Tower, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, and Copyist Hall Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.
S4 / Refugee Commons must preserve Refugee Commons Civic Hall, Aid Commons, Woven Banners Monument, and Ration Arcade Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.
S5 / Nursery / Spawning Grounds must preserve Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, Nursery Basin, Shell Inlay Monument, and Spawning Pool Nook as the source-backed anchor cluster.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Show Volgrad as a Eastern European / Slavic city using Blue Granite, Frost Glass, Pine, Silver Inlay, Arcane Glow across Dry Stone Paving, Civic Terraces.
Keep Eastern Watchfire Kremlin of the Frost Gate as the skyline-scale Wonder, Frost Rune Edges Sovereign Registry Court as central D13 authority, and Guildhall Beacon as the public signal landmark.
Use section landmark distribution to avoid flattening the city: Artisan Guilds, High Residential, Records / Archives, Refugee Commons, Nursery / Spawning Grounds.
Water and terrain must follow source context: dry city ground; Dry Stone Paving, Civic Terraces.
requiredVisualElements: Eastern Watchfire Kremlin of the Frost Gate
Frost Rune Edges Sovereign Registry Court
elementsToAvoid: soft pastoral warmth as dominant tone
invented district-specific landmarks not supported by sidecars
replacing proper anchor names with generic summaries
collapsing five section spines into one repeated street type
